name: Fathom Analytics API
what_it_does: Fathom Analytics — privacy-first web analytics with a REST API for querying aggregated site metrics, page views, and custom events without tracking individuals or using cookies.
use_cases: Agents generating weekly traffic reports from Fathom's aggregated metrics without compliance concerns
Conversion tracking automation — agents querying Fathom event completions to calculate conversion rates automatically
Multi-site analytics aggregation — agents pulling metrics across multiple sites for portfolio dashboards
Revenue attribution — agents correlating Fathom referrer data with Stripe payment data for marketing ROI analysis
Content performance scoring — agents ranking pages by engagement metrics to guide content strategy
not_for: User-level behavioral analytics — Fathom is aggregate-only by design
Funnel analysis with individual user journeys — use PostHog or Mixpanel for user-level funnels
High-frequency real-time analytics pipelines — Fathom's API is for reporting, not streaming
best_when: You need simple, GDPR-compliant web analytics with a clean API for automated reporting and no cookie consent banner required.
avoid_when: You need user-level tracking, session replay, or complex funnel analysis — Plausible or PostHog are better fits.

auth.notes: API token from Fathom account. Bearer token in Authorization header. Account-level token — accesses all sites. Token rotation supported.

pricing.notes: No free tier — all plans are paid. Simple pricing based on pageview volume. Includes all features in every plan. No feature tiers.

performance.rate_limits: API: no published rate limits; reporting throttled for extreme query volumes

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean REST API with good documentation. Error responses are descriptive. No complex pagination — simple date range filtering. Consistent response format.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All API endpoints are read-only — completely idempotent. Event ingestion is via JS pixel, not REST API.
known_agent_gotchas: API is read-only — agents cannot create events or pages via REST API; tracking requires JS snippet
Fathom uses intelligent EU routing — data is processed in EU by default, affecting latency from non-EU regions
Custom event goals must be created in dashboard before they appear in API responses
Historical data is limited by account age — agents querying before account creation date get empty results
No public rate limit documentation — agents should implement conservative backoff for high-frequency polling

security_score_components.security_notes: Privacy-first architecture — no personal data stored. GDPR compliant by design. EU data residency. No SOC2 but strong privacy posture. Cookieless tracking eliminates consent compliance burden.
